notes.note: The plainest of Cicero's salutations to Cornificius: S. is salutem (dicit), "sends greetings". Unlike 12.17 and 12.18 it omits CONLEGAE.

notes.anchor: Caesar has assigned the province of Syria to you
notes.note: As dictator, Caesar allotted provincial commands directly; here he sends Cornificius to Syria to deal with the revolt of Caecilius Bassus and the Parthian threat on the eastern frontier.
The Parthian empire beyond the Euphrates was Rome's great eastern rival; after the disaster at Carrhae (53 BC) a Parthian invasion of Syria was a standing danger for any governor there.

M. Calpurnius Bibulus, Caesar's consular colleague of 59 BC, governed Syria in 51–50 BC and, lacking forces, simply shut himself up in a fortified town until the raiding Parthians withdrew; Cicero recommends the same Fabian caution.
